items.title: The immersive Indigenous language class where English is left at the door

children.content: After learning from Native American Yuchi families, Pertame elders and young mothers in Alice Springs are raising babies in a total immersion Language Nest and hope to extend the model into primary school.

gallery.altText: A woman plays blocks with a child.

thumbnail.altText: A woman plays blocks with a child.

items.title: NT's deputy opposition leader dumped for new recruit

children.content: The Northern Territory Labor party has replaced its deputy opposition leader with a newcomer to parliament, in a move they say will win back the trust and confidence of the people of Darwin.

image.altText: A man wearing a red shirt standing in a shed, looking directly down the camera.

items.title: NT government extends town camp alcohol bans

children.content: The NT government is extending alcohol bans in town camps beyond 2027, in a move welcomed by the health sector as a win for community safety.

image.altText: A row of empty liquor bottles lined up on a kitchen bench, the adjoining walls are covered in marks and dents.

items.title: In pictures: Alice Springs celebrates 30 years of unique craft festival

children.content: As the Alice Springs Beanie Festival celebrates its 30th and final year, we're exploring the weird and wonderful handmade creations — and the characters working hard behind the scenes — that have made the annual event so special.

gallery.altText: A man smiling at the camera, on his head is a handmade beanie featuring a knitted monkey holding a banana.
A group of women wearing colourful knit beanies, smiling as they take a photo together.
A boy holds a colourful knitted fish while wearing a handmade beanie, which has small yellow creature stitched on the top.

A group of women wearing colourful knit beanies, smiling as they take a photo together.
A boy holds a colourful knitted fish while wearing a handmade beanie, which has small yellow creature stitched on the top.

avatar.alt: Victoria Ellis, a brunette, curly haired woman leans one elbow on a fence and smiles at the camera.

items.title: Trump says Reflecting Pool may be drained for repairs after 'vandalism'

children.content: Donald Trump says the newly revamped Reflecting Pool needs "repairs" and that multiple people have been arrested for vandalising the Washington DC water feature.

A man standing waist-deep in a green poll, holding a large cleaning tool

items.title: Opposition backs Israel's right to military action in Lebanon

children.content: The Shadow Foreign Affairs Minister has defended Israel’s right to continue its military operation in Lebanon, despite an MOU between the US and Iran which commits to an end to the war.

image.altText: Ted O’Brien at a press conference.

items.title: Why fuel excise cut is being extended by another month but at smaller discount

children.content: The fuel excise cut was due to stop at the end of the month but will continue to a lesser extent until August. Here's why.

image.altText: Two hands on a premium petrol pump.

items.title: Bird flu detection 'a distant concern' for Victoria, industry says

children.content: The head of Victoria's peak farming group says the detection of the deadly H5 variant of avian influenza in Western Australia is a "distant concern" for Victorians.

image.altText: Dozens of hens with glossy brown feathers and red plump combs under a clear blue sky.

items.title: They bought into Australia's tallest tower. Now their deposits are in limbo

children.content: Apartment buyers who were sold on ambitious plans for the tallest building in the country say they're facing up to a decade of uncertainty after the head-turning project faltered.

image.altText: a mock up image of what the tower would look like when complete.

items.title: Mona unveils $100m underground library packed with rare literary treasures

children.content: From Shakespeare's First Folio to neon-lit shelves to a curvy kids' section, Mona's new library, Phrontisterion,
is anything but ordinary.

Room with curved seating and roof design.

items.title: James Tedesco in the wars as Roosters try to hold off Sharks

children.content: Referee Ashley Klein asks Roosters captain James Tedesco to clean some of the blood off his face before speaking to him during the second half of the Roosters' clash with the Sharks. Follow live.

image.altText: A bloodied James Tedesco speaks to referee Ashley Klein.

items.title: Bulldogs get the points at Docklands amid big injury worries for Saints

children.content: The Western Bulldogs get the points at Docklands against St Kilda to finish round 15, as the Saints lose two key players to injury.

image.altText: A Western Bulldogs tall forward is surrounded by teammates congratulating him after a vital goal.

items.title: Quick hits: Snakes at Germany's training and a player nicks a ref's watch

children.content: A Curaçao goalkeeper etches out a piece of World Cup history, Germany faces some slippery training pitch invaders and a referee's watch is briefly nicked by a player. Here are today's quick hits.

articles.title: Japan blitzes hapless Tunisia 4-0 as Curaçao makes World Cup history

children.content: Japan eases past Tunisia 4-0 in the 1,000th game at a men's World Cup after Curaçao goalkeeper Eloy Room produces an inspired performance to deliver his nation its first-ever point.

image.altText: Daichi Kamada holds his hand up to his face like a phone in celebration of a goal

articles.title: Is the most expensive World Cup in history worth the cost for travelling fans?

children.content: There has been endless talk about fans paying high prices for this World Cup. So is it worth it? And is it time to accept the people's game is no longer within everyone's reach?

image.altText: A composite image of several World Cup fans from different countries with face paint

gallery.altText: Quick Hits artwork June 21

thumbnail.altText: Quick Hits artwork June 21

items.title: Thunderbirds first team through to Super Netball Grand Final

children.content: The Adelaide Thunderbirds are through to their third grand final in four years. Now they wait to see if they will face the Melbourne Vixens or West Coast Fever for a shot at the title.

image.altText: Players hug and smile as they celebrate in a huddle

items.title: WA football great Cousins taking inspiration from Daniher in MND fight

children.content: WA football great Bryan Cousins is taking inspiration from the late Neale Daniher in his fight with motor neurone disease, as he pays tribute to son Ben for his efforts in combating addiction.

image.altText: Close-up of a man with short grey hair

items.title: How many clothes should you own? The maths behind a sustainable wardrobe

children.content: The environmental impact of a garment often depends on how many times it's worn. In a sense, every additional wear helps "offset" the garment's carbon footprint.

items.title: Love story: Coming out as a throuple

children.content: Everyone is bisexual, polyamorous, and has a crush on me. Or at least, that's my default assumption and the pick-up line that led to my triad with Rhea and Leo.

image.altText: An illustration of three people sitting together.

items.title: Is 'baby brain' real? A neuroscientist explains

children.content: The fog new parents feel is real, but it seems to be driven less by motherhood rewiring the brain than by exhaustion, overload and the relentless demands of caring for a tiny human.

image.altText: A close up photo of a woman hugging and kissing a baby in a navy striped top

children.content: A walk along the beach with a friend turned into the significant discovery.

items.title: Changes to support regional Queensland patients a 'drop in the ocean'

children.content: The Queensland government is spruiking its cost-of-living measures in its upcoming state budget, but advocates say changes to support regional patients are disappointing.

image.altText: A woman in a green jumper leans on a fence post outside for a portrait.

items.title: Police speech stuns descendants gathered at site of outback mass killing

items.title: The people bringing life back to Maryborough's heritage buildings

children.content: Some Maryborough residents are secretly restoring several heritage-listed buildings to their original state, transforming them in a bid to create an entertainment and dining precinct in  the regional Queensland city.

items.title: Why a trip to Asia might be a good idea now

children.content: The war in the Middle East has not stopped Australians travelling but their preferences may be changing.

items.title: War helped 're-enliven' Iran's regime. Trump's truce may complicate that

children.content: For decades the Islamic republic has been using its "main enemy" America to distract from its repressive regime, analysts say. But the recent truce with Donald Trump could change that.

items.title: The quest for the truth behind Australian nurses' massacre in WWII

children.content: A relative of one of the 21 Australian nurses massacred by Japanese soldiers on a remote Indonesian beach in WWII wants the real story of what happened to be told.

items.title: Al Jazeera cameraman killed in Israeli strike in Gaza

items.title: Surprise gift leaves Aboriginal war veteran 'pleased as bloody punch'

children.content: It took Wiradjuri man Victor Bartley 16 years to be recognised for his military service. Now, 55 years after he came home, a charity has tracked him down to offer him thanks.

items.title: Meloni tells Trump to 'focus on your own popularity'
